title: TonalityISO1996_2 > TonalityISO1996_2 > TonalityISO1996_2
text:
class ansys.sound.core.psychoacoustics.TonalityISO1996_2(signal=None, noise_pause_threshold=1.0, effective_analysis_bandwidth=5.0, noise_bandwidth_ratio=0.75)

Computes the tonality according to the standard ISO 1996-2:2007, annex C.

This class is used to compute the tonal audibility and tonal adjustment of a signal according
to the annex C of the ISO 1996-2:2007 standard.

!! processed by numpydoc !!

Class instantiation takes the following parameters.

Parameters

signal: Field, default: None

Signal in Pa on which to calculate the tonality.

noise_pause_threshold: float, default: 1.0

Noise pause detection threshold (“level excess”) in dB.

effective_analysis_bandwidth: float, default: 5.0

Effective analysis bandwidth in Hz.

noise_bandwidth_ratio: float, default: 0.75

Noise bandwidth, in proportion to the critical bandwidth, that is taken into account
for the calculation of the masking noise level (the default value 0.75 means that the
masking noise level is estimated in a band delimited by 75 % of the critical bandwidth
on each side of the tone). Value must be between 0.75 and 2.

For more information about the parameters, please refer to the Ansys Sound SAS user guide.

title: IsolateOrders
text:
Isolates the orders of a signal.
This class isolates the order of a signal that has an associated RPM profile.
Class instantiation takes the following parameters.
One or more input signals on which to isolate orders.
RPM signal associated with the time-domain signals.
It is assumed that the signal’s unit is rpm. If this is not the case,
inaccurate behavior might occur during the conversion from RPM to frequency.
List of the order numbers to isolate. The list must contain at least one value.
Size of the FFT used to compute the STFT.
Window type used for the FFT computation. Options are 'TRIANGULAR', 'BLACKMAN',
'BLACKMANHARRIS', 'HAMMING', 'HANN', 'GAUSS', 'FLATTOP',
and 'RECTANGULAR'.
Overlap value between two successive FFT computations. Values can range from 0 to 1.
For example, 0 means no overlap, and 0.5 means 50% overlap.
Width in Hz of the area used to select each individual order.
Note that its precision depends on the FFT size.

class ansys.sound.core.spectrogram_processing.IsolateOrders(signal=None, rpm_profile=None, orders=None, fft_size=1024, window_type='HANN', window_overlap=0.5, width_selection=10)

Isolates the orders of a signal.

This class isolates the order of a signal that has an associated RPM profile.

!! processed by numpydoc !!

Class instantiation takes the following parameters.

Parameters

signal

FieldsContainer | Field, default: python:None

One or more input signals on which to isolate orders.

rpm_profile

Field, default: python:None

RPM signal associated with the time-domain signals.
It is assumed that the signal’s unit is rpm. If this is not the case,
inaccurate behavior might occur during the conversion from RPM to frequency.

orders

python:list, default: python:None

List of the order numbers to isolate. The list must contain at least one value.

fft_size

python:int, default: 1024

Size of the FFT used to compute the STFT.

window_type

python:str, default: ‘HANN’

Window type used for the FFT computation. Options are 'TRIANGULAR', 'BLACKMAN',
'BLACKMANHARRIS', 'HAMMING', 'HANN', 'GAUSS', 'FLATTOP',

and 'RECTANGULAR'.

window_overlap

python:float, default: 0.5

Overlap value between two successive FFT computations. Values can range from 0 to 1.
For example, 0 means no overlap, and 0.5 means 50% overlap.

width_selection

python:int, default: 10

Width in Hz of the area used to select each individual order.
Note that its precision depends on the FFT size.

title: connect_to_or_start_server
text:
Connect to or start a DPF server with the DPF Sound plugin loaded.
:rtype: Any
If a port or IP address is set, this method tries to connect to the server specified
and the ansys_path parameter is ignored. If no parameters are set, a local server
from the latest available Ansys installation is started.
Port that the DPF server is listening to.
IP address for the DPF server.
Root path for the Ansys installation. For example, C:\\Program Files\\ANSYS Inc\\v242.
This parameter is ignored if either the port or IP address is set.
Whether to check out the DPF Sound license increment (avrxp_snd_level1) before using
PyAnsys Sound. Checking out the license increment improves performance if you are doing
multiple calls to DPF Sound operators because they require licensing. This parameter
can also be used to force check out before running a script when few DPF Sound license
increments are available. The license is checked in when the server object is deleted.
If a different license increment is required, set this parameter to True and set the
license_increment_name parameter to the name of the license increment to check out.
Name of the license increment to check out. Only taken into account if use_license_context
is True. The default value is avrxp_snd_level1, which corresponds to the license
required by Ansys Sound Pro.

title: connect_to_or_start_server > connect_to_or_start_server > connect_to_or_start_server
text:
ansys.sound.core.server_helpers._connect_to_or_start_server.connect_to_or_start_server(port=None, ip=None, ansys_path=None, use_license_context=False, license_increment_name='avrxp_snd_level1')

Connect to or start a DPF server with the DPF Sound plugin loaded.
:rtype: Any

If a port or IP address is set, this method tries to connect to the server specified
and the ansys_path parameter is ignored. If no parameters are set, a local server
from the latest available Ansys installation is started.

Parameters

port

python:int, default: python:None

Port that the DPF server is listening to.

ip

python:str, default: python:None

IP address for the DPF server.

ansys_path

python:str, default: python:None

Root path for the Ansys installation. For example, C:\\Program Files\\ANSYS Inc\\v242.
This parameter is ignored if either the port or IP address is set.

use_license_context

bool, default: python:False

Whether to check out the DPF Sound license increment (avrxp_snd_level1) before using
PyAnsys Sound. Checking out the license increment improves performance if you are doing
multiple calls to DPF Sound operators because they require licensing. This parameter
can also be used to force check out before running a script when few DPF Sound license
increments are available. The license is checked in when the server object is deleted.
If a different license increment is required, set this parameter to True and set the
license_increment_name parameter to the name of the license increment to check out.

license_increment_name

python:str, default: “avrxp_snd_level1”

Name of the license increment to check out. Only taken into account if use_license_context
is True. The default value is avrxp_snd_level1, which corresponds to the license
required by Ansys Sound Pro.

Returns

Any

server : server.ServerBase
